document.title: Librarian2 Registry Editor Design
document.purpose: Describe the design and behavior of the Librarian2 Registry Editor application, a schema-aware tool for creating and editing Librarian2 resource registry files.
document.author: Lion + ChatGPT

overview.description: The Librarian2 Registry Editor is a schema-aware JSON editor specialized for Librarian2 registry files. Its purpose is to make editing registry files fast, safe, and pleasant while preserving the flexibility and openness of the registry schema.
core_goals: Make registry editing fast and intuitive
Gracefully handle unknown or future schema extensions
overview.guiding_principle: Typed editors when possible, raw JSON when necessary.

ui_layout.description: The editor uses a two-pane layout with an entry index on the left and a resource editor on the right.

left_pane.name: registry_entries
left_pane.description: Displays a list of resource entries contained in the registry.
features: Optional display of logical and semantic types

right_pane.name: resource_editor
right_pane.description: Displays the editable contents of the selected resource entry.

entry_index.description: The entry index lists all resources in the registry.

typed_editor.description: The typed editor provides structured editing controls for known fields and recognized type patterns.

location_editor.description: Allows editing and creation of location descriptors.

raw_json_editor.description: Displays the full JSON representation of the resource entry.
purpose: Guarantee that any valid registry can be edited

type_aware_editing.description: The editor inspects the type field and activates specialized editing interfaces when possible.
type_aware_editing.fallback_behavior: If a type is not recognized, the editor falls back to the raw JSON editor.

location_editor.description: Location descriptors are edited as open-ended objects.

custom_descriptor_support.description: Users may define arbitrary location descriptor keys.

location_editor.principle: The editor must allow arbitrary descriptor fields so that future protocols and access methods can be represented without schema changes.

entry_creation.description: Creating a new entry should require minimal information.

positional_entries.description: The editor supports positional convenience entries.

rules: These entries are not stable identifiers
They should not be referenced by other entries

registry_navigation.description: Future versions of the editor may allow navigation between registries.

safety_features.description: Because registry files are important infrastructure, the editor should provide safety mechanisms.
safety_features.important_rule: The editor must not enforce strict validation that would prevent editing of registries containing unknown or extended fields.

technology_considerations.description: The editor is expected to integrate naturally with Lion's existing tool ecosystem.

architectural_observation.description: The Librarian2 registry editor functions as a navigation and management interface for a developer's resource ecosystem.
architectural_observation.analogy: It behaves similarly to a package index browser, but operates locally and describes arbitrary resources rather than packages.
